instruction: What is the cwe of the cve with description ': Relative Path Traversal vulnerability in B&R Industrial Automation Automation Studio allows Relative Path Traversal.This issue affects Automation Studio: from 4.0 through 4.12.'?
output:
MATCH (cve:CVE)
WHERE toLower(cve.description) CONTAINS toLower('Relative Path Traversal vulnerability')
  AND toLower(cve.description) CONTAINS toLower('B&R Industrial Automation Automation Studio')
  AND toLower(cve.description) CONTAINS toLower('allows Relative Path Traversal')
  AND toLower(cve.description) CONTAINS toLower('from 4.0 through 4.12')
OPTIONAL MATCH (cve)-[:HAS_CWE]->(cwe:CWE)
RETURN cwe;
---
MATCH (cve:CVE)
WHERE toLower(cve.description) CONTAINS toLower('Relative Path Traversal vulnerability')
  AND toLower(cve.description) CONTAINS toLower('B&R Industrial Automation Automation Studio')
  AND toLower(cve.description) CONTAINS toLower('allows Relative Path Traversal')
  AND toLower(cve.description) CONTAINS toLower('from 4.0 through 4.12')
OPTIONAL MATCH (cve)-[:HAS_POSSIBLE_CWE]->(cwe:CWE)
RETURN cwe;

instruction: What is the cwe of the cve with description 'BuildKit is a toolkit for converting source code to build artifacts in an efficient, expressive and repeatable manner. A malicious BuildKit frontend or Dockerfile using RUN --mount could trick the feature that removes empty files created for the mountpoints into removing a file outside the container, from the host system. The issue has been fixed in v0.12.5. Workarounds include avoiding using BuildKit frontends from an untrusted source or building an untrusted Dockerfile containing RUN --mount feature.'?
output:
MATCH (cve:CVE)
WHERE toLower(cve.description) CONTAINS toLower('BuildKit is a toolkit for converting source code')
  AND toLower(cve.description) CONTAINS toLower('malicious BuildKit frontend or Dockerfile using RUN --mount')
  AND toLower(cve.description) CONTAINS toLower('removing a file outside the container')
  AND toLower(cve.description) CONTAINS toLower('fixed in v0.12.5')
OPTIONAL MATCH (cve)-[:HAS_CWE]->(cwe:CWE)
RETURN cwe;
---
MATCH (cve:CVE)
WHERE toLower(cve.description) CONTAINS toLower('BuildKit is a toolkit for converting source code')
  AND toLower(cve.description) CONTAINS toLower('malicious BuildKit frontend or Dockerfile using RUN --mount')
  AND toLower(cve.description) CONTAINS toLower('removing a file outside the container')
  AND toLower(cve.description) CONTAINS toLower('fixed in v0.12.5')
OPTIONAL MATCH (cve)-[:HAS_POSSIBLE_CWE]->(cwe:CWE)
RETURN cwe;

instruction: What is the cwe of the cve with description 'A vulnerability, which was classified as critical, has been found in Kashipara Food Management System up to 1.0. The exploit has been disclosed to the public and may be used. The identifier VDB-249833 was assigned to this vulnerability.'?

instruction: What is the cwe of the cve with description 'Ursa is a cryptographic library for use with blockchains. A weakness in the Hyperledger AnonCreds specification that is not mitigated in the Ursa and AnonCreds implementations is that the Issuer does not publish a key correctness proof demonstrating that a generated private key is sufficient to meet the unlinkability guarantees of AnonCreds. The Ursa and AnonCreds CL-Signatures implementations always generate a sufficient private key. A malicious issuer could in theory create a custom CL Signature implementation (derived from the Ursa or AnonCreds CL-Signatures implementations) that uses weakened private keys such that presentations from holders could be shared by verifiers to the issuer who could determine the holder to which the credential was issued. This vulnerability could impact holders of AnonCreds credentials implemented using the CL-signature scheme in the Ursa and AnonCreds implementations of CL Signatures. The ursa project has has moved to end-of-life status and no fix is expected.'?

OPTIONAL MATCH (cve)-[:HAS_CWE]->(cwe:CWE)

instruction: Locate the path between MQTT_Broker_1 and Safety_PLC_2.
output:
MATCH (e:Product) WHERE e.name IN ["MQTT_Broker_1"]
WITH collect(id(e)) AS entryIds
MATCH (t:Product) WHERE t.name IN ["Safety_PLC_2"]
WITH entryIds, collect(id(t)) AS targetIds

UNWIND entryIds AS s
UNWIND targetIds AS t
CALL gds.shortestPath.yens.stream('attack_graph', {
  sourceNode: s,
  targetNode: t,
  k: 10
})
YIELD path
WITH path,
     head(nodes(path)) AS srcNode,
     last(nodes(path)) AS dstNode,
     [n IN nodes(path) | coalesce(n.name, n.product_id, elementId(n))] AS product_hops,
     size(relationships(path)) AS hop_count
RETURN
  coalesce(srcNode.name, srcNode.product_id, elementId(srcNode)) AS source,
  coalesce(dstNode.name, dstNode.product_id, elementId(dstNode)) AS target,
  hop_count,
  product_hops
ORDER BY hop_count DESC LIMIT 5;
